title: Bendix Fust

{{Quote|Bendix Fust: wanted alive. Currently serving time on Oovo IV in the asteroid prison. Reward is 50,000 on delivery.|Rozatta to Jango Fett|<ref name="BH" />}}
'''Bendix Fust''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Mordageen]], employed as a [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] by the [[Dug/Legends|Dug]] [[King/Legends|King]] [[Sebolto]] in the final decades of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. Fust smuggled [[Death stick/Legends|death sticks]], illegal euphoria-inducing drugs, until he was captured by members of the [[Republic Security Force/Legends|Republic Security Force]] in the [[Gazzari system/Legends|Gazzari system]]. Fust struck a deal with the agency, trading information on [[Sebolto's drug cartel]] in exchange for a reduced [[Prison/Legends|prison]] sentence. When Sebolto found out, he placed a [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] on Fust's head; the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]] [[Zam Wesell/Legends|Zam Wesell]] and [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]] claimed the bounty by breaking the Mordageen out of the [[Desolation Alley]] maximum security prison and handing him over to the Dug.

==Biography==
[[File:Bendixfust.jpg|thumb|left|upright|Bendix Fust in his prison uniform.]]
Bendix Fust was a [[Mordageen]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] from the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Mordagon]].<ref name="Prima" /> He was employed by the [[Dug/Legends|Dug]] [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] [[Sebolto]] as a [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]], transporting illegal drugs known as [[Death stick/Legends|death sticks]]. While working in the [[Gazzari system/Legends|Gazzari system]], Fust was captured by members of the [[Republic Security Force/Legends|Republic Security Force]] in a sting operation against Sebolto's [[Sebolto's drug cartel|drug cartel]]. Faced with a long term of imprisonment, the Mordageen made a deal with the agency, telling them all he knew of Sebolto's drug cartel in exchange for a reduced prison sentence. He was then interred into a maximum security [[Prison/Legends|prison]] on the [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroid]] [[Oovo IV/Legends|Oovo IV]],<ref name="BH" /> a facility known as [[Desolation Alley]],<ref name="Racer">[[Star Wars: Episode I Racer|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''Racer'']]</ref> and was assigned prisoner number 004563-013.<ref name="Gamer">{{GamerCite|10|State of the Arts}}</ref>

Once Sebolto discovered that Fust had sold him out, he posted a 50,000-[[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credit]] [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] for anyone who brought Fust to him alive.<ref name="BH" /> In [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]],<ref name="Date 32 BBY">''[[Star Wars: Bounty Hunter]]'' is set immediatly after the [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|Invasion of Naboo]], which ''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' dates to [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]].</ref> this offer was taken up independently by two [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]], [[Zam Wesell/Legends|Zam Wesell]] and [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]], who was told about the bounty by [[Toydarian/Legends|Toydarian]] [[Rozatta]]. Wesell, a [[Changeling/Legends|shapeshifting]] [[Clawdite/Legends|Clawdite]], stowed away aboard a supply vessel and infiltrated the prison. She arrived at Fust's cell first, and when he refused to accompany her, the Clawdite used a [[Gas grenade/Legends|gas grenade]] to subdue him. She also initiated a prison riot to cover her escape.<ref name="BH" />

Shortly after Wesell left, the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] bounty hunter Jango Fett arrived at the cell, having traversed the prison's ventilation shafts. Fett followed after the escaping pair, eventually catching up with them just as they reached his [[Jaster's Legacy|starship]], which Wesell hoped to hijack to escape the prison. Faced with numerous prison guards, the two professionals opted to join forces to escape the prison, splitting the bounty on Fust's head. Although Fett's ship was destroyed before they could escape in it, the pair managed to hijack a [[Firespray-31-class patrol and attack craft/Legends|''Firespray''-class]] [[Slave I/Legends|patrol ship]] and leave Oovo. The bounty hunters then took Fust to [[Malastare/Legends|Malastare]], Sebolto's homeworld where Fett allowed Wesell to hand Fust over to Sebolto at his [[Sebolto's Palace|palace]], using the occasion to sneak into the Palace for his own purposes.<ref name="BH" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|Blast me? That's nothing compared to what Sebolto will do. I ain't going back to Malastare, no way.|Fust to Zam Wesell|<ref name="BH" />}}
Fust smuggled death sticks,<ref name="BH" /> an illegal drug composed of treated [[Ixetal cilona/Legends|Ixetal cilona]] extract that offers euphoria in exchange for a shortened life;<ref name="Hnn">{{HoloNetNewsWeb|49|life/13328_1.html|SPECIAL REPORT: The Death Stick Scourge}}</ref> consequently, upon being captured by Republic forces, he was to be imprisoned for a significant period of time. In this situation, Fust was prepared to sell out his employer in exchange for a reduced prison sentence.<ref name="BH" />

After selling out his boss, Fust was very afraid of what Sebolto would do to him in reparation. Consequently, when Zam Wesell broke into his cell, he preferred to stay in prison rather than be returned to the Dug.<ref name="BH" /> Fust had [[Color/Legends|white]] [[Hair/Legends|hair]]<ref name="Prima" /> and pink skin.<ref name="BH" />

==Equipment==
While in prison, Fust wore a red prison [[Clothing/Legends|uniform]] and black shoes.<ref name="BH" />

==Behind the scenes==
Fust was first mentioned by the reference book ''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]'',<ref name="Characters">''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]''</ref> published on [[April 23]], [[2002]],<ref name="Publish Date">{{SWArchive|url=eu/lit/novel/news20020212.html|text=Book Preview: The New Essential Guide}}</ref> Fust first appeared in ''[[Star Wars: Bounty Hunter]]'', a [[Star Wars video games|video game]] produced by [[LucasArts]]<ref name="BH" /> and released for the [[Wikipedia:PlayStation 2|PlayStation 2]] on [[November 19]], 2002.<ref name="PS2 Date>{{WebCite|url=http://www.gamefaqs.com/ps2/557901-star-wars-bounty-hunter/data|text=GameFAQs Title Data for ''Star Wars: Bounty Hunter''|work=GameFAQs|archivedate=20160304090346}}</ref> In the game, Fust was voiced by [[Wally Wingert]], who also voiced another character, [[Jervis Gloom]].<ref name="BH" />
